subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Life Detection Technology
description: A micro-lesson that makes Life Detection Technology usable.

hook.text: Finding alien life needs tools that can detect it. Scientists are building instruments to spot life in forms we've never seen.

buildup.text: Traditional biosignature detection looks for specific molecules. But alien life might use different chemistry entirely.

discovery.text: Assembly theory measures molecular complexity. Life produces complex molecules that random chemistry almost never creates.

twist.text: This approach is agnostic—it doesn't assume carbon, water, or DNA. It just looks for the fingerprint of any living system.

climax.text: Future landers may carry these instruments to Europa or Enceladus—testing for life without assuming what it looks like.

punchline.text: Detect complexity, not specific molecules. A universal life-finding machine.

quiz.question: What is the advantage of assembly theory for detecting alien life?
options: It detects molecular complexity without assuming life uses specific chemistry like DNA or carbon
It can detect life from millions of light-years away using telescopes
It tests for the presence of water, which all life requires
